free_text: Same values given
Neutron activation cross sections on Nd isotopes
at 14.8 MeV
Nd2O3 powder pressed into plexiglass containers of
diameter 16 mm. Natural and enriched samples used,
their isotopic abundances are given in % in the
following Table.
Sample              Isotope
Average of both monitor reactions was used for
calibration of neutron flux. Numerical values actually
used at 14.8 MeV not given, only references.
Al(n,p)
Fe-56(n,p)
Authors found that gamma-intensities from different
references are in many cases inconsistent and represent
one major source of errors for activation cross
sections. For each (n,2n) and (n,p) reaction several
gamma lines were measured and a set of intensities was
chosen which was found to be the most consistent. For
comparison with other works the gamma-intensities used
have always to be considered.
Data from Acta Phys.Slov.33(1983)9,Tables 2,3,4
Approved by Ribansky 19/04/85
OS.
DG.-Correction after author approval.-
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Data corrected for time variations of neutron flux,
coincidence summing effects and gamma self-absorption.
No information given
Deduced
(sum of metastable and ground state)
654.8 keV gamma line was measured but disregarded
because cross section obtained was always inconsistent
with the two other lines measured (with intensity sets
from 3 different references).
Sum of ground + metastable state measured directly
Authors tried to determine sig(metastable)
separately by decay curve analysis but found no
significant yield of sig(metastable). (Less than
5% of sig(total).
Deduced
Sum of metastable + ground state
sig(ground) and sig(metastable) both determined by
decay curve analysis of the 696.5 keV line.
These gamma lines gave cross sections differing by
32%. Result given is mean value.
Sum of ground + metastable state measured directly.
Sig(metastable) could not be obtained because the
isomeric transition could not be measured
(energy and half-life very close to a line
produced in the (n,2n) reaction) and a decay
curve analysis of the 165.8 keV line was unsuccessful.
